nodes.name: Sticky Note38

parameters.content:
## Convert URL HTML to Markdown and Get Page Links

## Use Case
Transform web pages into AI-friendly markdown format:
- You need to process webpage content for LLM analysis
- You want to extract both content and links from web pages
- You need clean, formatted text without HTML markup
- You want to respect API rate limits while crawling pages

## What this Workflow Does
The workflow uses Firecrawl.dev API to process webpages:
- Converts HTML content to markdown format
- Extracts all links from each webpage
- Handles API rate limiting automatically
- Processes URLs in batches from your database

## Setup
1. Create a [Firecrawl.dev]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} account and get your API key
2. Add your Firecrawl API key to the HTTP Request node's Authorization header
3. Connect your URL database to the input node (column name must be "Page") or edit the array in `Example fields from data source`
4. Configure your preferred output database connection

## How to Adjust it to Your Needs
- Modify input source to pull URLs from different databases
- Adjust rate limiting parameters if needed
- Customize output format for your specific use case


Made by Simon @ [automake.io]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

parameters.content: **Firecrawl.dev retrieves markdown inc. title, description, links & content. First define the URLs you'd like to scrape**

**REQUIRED**
Connect to your database of urls to input. Name the column `Page` like in the `Example fields from data source` node and make sure it has one link per row like `split out page urls`

**REQUIRED**
Update the Auth parameter to your own [Firecrawl]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} dev token

**Header Auth parameter**
name - Authorization
value - your-own-api-key

**REQUIRED** 
Output the data to your own data source e.g. Airtable
**40 at a time seems to be the memory limit on my server - run until complete with batches of 40 or increase based on your server memory**

## Convert URL HTML to Markdown and Get Page Links

## Use Case
Transform web pages into AI-friendly markdown format:
- You need to process webpage content for LLM analysis
- You want to extract both content and links from web pages
- You need clean, formatted text without HTML markup
- You want to respect API rate limits while crawling pages

## What this Workflow Does
The workflow uses Firecrawl.dev API to process webpages:
- Converts HTML content to markdown format
- Extracts all links from each webpage
- Handles API rate limiting automatically
- Processes URLs in batches from your database

## Setup
1. Create a [Firecrawl.dev]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} account and get your API key
2. Add your Firecrawl API key to the HTTP Request node's Authorization header
3. Connect your URL database to the input node (column name must be "Page") or edit the array in `Example fields from data source`
4. Configure your preferred output database connection

## How to Adjust it to Your Needs
- Modify input source to pull URLs from different databases
- Adjust rate limiting parameters if needed
- Customize output format for your specific use case


Made by Simon @ [automake.io]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking ‘Test workflow’".
Open the "Wait" node (n8n-nodes-base.wait) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note36" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note28" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Connect to your own data source" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get urls from own data source" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note33" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note34" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note35" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note37" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "40 items at a time" node (n8n-nodes-base.limit) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "10 at a time" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Split out page URLs" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitOut) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Retrieve Page Markdown and Links" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note38" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Example fields from data source".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Markdown data and Links".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
